title: A Visit to the Apple Farm
hint: Compound sentences with and/but, past tense verbs, silent-e words.

content.text: Yesterday, my family visited a huge apple farm, and we picked many sweet red apples. We wanted to bake a fresh pie, but we did not have enough white sugar. My kind mother walked to the small shop nearby. She bought a bag of sugar, and we made a wonderful pie together.

title: Comprehension: A Visit to the Apple Farm

title: The Lost Kitten in the Rain
hint: Compound sentences, vowel teams (rain, gray, cried, afraid), and irregular past tense.

content.text: A tiny gray kitten sat under a green bench in the rain, and it cried sadly because it was wet. I wanted to help, but the kitten was too afraid to come to me. I walked home and brought some warm milk, and then the sweet kitten ran to me. We are happy now.

title: Comprehension: The Lost Kitten in the Rain

title: Fun at the Sandy Beach
hint: Check vowel teams (beach, waves, seashells) and irregular plurals.

content.text: Last Saturday, we drove to the beautiful beach to swim. We built a giant sandcastle near the water, but the big ocean waves washed it away quickly. We did not cry, and instead, we looked for pretty seashells. We found five shiny blue shells and three tiny red crabs.

title: The Story of the Clever Crow
hint: Silent-e/vowel teams (crow, pitcher, low, thought, rose, drank) and compound structures.

title: Winter Snow and Warm Cocoa
hint: Look for vowel teams (snow, cocoa, fireplace, stayed, cookies, watching) and compound sentences.
